summary.intent: Implement application-level HTTP/SOCKS5 proxy support for the moltis project (issue #548), allowing users behind corporate firewalls to route all outbound traffic through a configured proxy.
summary.outcome: Full upstream proxy support implemented across providers, tools, OAuth, MCP auth, Slack, and MS Teams via a new `upstream_proxy` config field (typed as `Secret<String>`). PR #561 created, reviewed, and iterated on with fixes for credential redaction in logs and `rfind('@')` correctness.

repo: Two shared HTTP clients exist: `moltis_tools::shared_http_client()` (had proxy plumbing for sandbox) and `moltis_providers::shared_http_client()` (used plain `Client::new()`). ~30+ places also created `reqwest::Client::new()` independently.

teloxide (Telegram) bundles reqwest 0.11 internally while the workspace uses reqwest 0.12 — `Bot::with_client()` expects a 0.11 Client, making cross-version proxy injection impossible without unsafe code.
Workspace lint denies `unsafe_code` (`unsafe_code = "deny"` at workspace level), so `std::env::set_var` cannot be used as a workaround in Rust 2024 edition.
The `just lint` and `just test` recipes use `--all-features` which pulls in CUDA/llama-cpp features that fail to build on macOS — pre-existing issue, not introduced here.
taplo auto-sorts Cargo.toml feature arrays alphabetically on format.
Secret fields use `deserialize_option_secret`/`serialize_option_secret` serde helpers — pattern already established in the codebase for other credential fields.
Slack's `chat.postMessage` uses `slack-morphism`'s hyper connector internally, which bypasses reqwest-based proxy injection entirely.
`build_schema_map()` in validate.rs defines the TOML schema for validation — new top-level config fields must be registered there.

The trusted-network (sandbox) proxy and upstream (corporate) proxy serve different purposes: sandbox proxy handles domain allowlisting for tools, upstream proxy handles external network routing for all clients.

code.finding: New module added to moltis-common housing build_http_client(), set_upstream_proxy_url(), upstream_proxy_url() global accessor, and redact_proxy_url() — shared by both moltis-tools and moltis-providers.
redact_proxy_url() must use rfind('@') not find('@') to correctly handle passwords containing literal '@' characters.
Gateway startup is the correct place to initialize both shared HTTP clients and set the global proxy URL — happens before channels/tasks spawn.
Telegram proxy injection is impossible at the code level due to reqwest 0.11/0.12 version mismatch with teloxide. Users must set HTTPS_PROXY env var manually.
upstream_proxy field typed as Option<Secret<String>> with deserialize_option_secret serde attribute to prevent credential leakage in debug output.

workflow: When reqwest is used by a vendored dependency at a different version (0.11 vs 0.12), you cannot inject types (like Proxy) across the version boundary — must fall back to env var approach or document the gap.
For shared utility functions needed by multiple crates without circular dependencies, adding to moltis-common is the right home — but requires adding reqwest as a dep to common.
PR review cycles caught: (1) credentials in log output, (2) missing Secret<> wrapping, (3) find vs rfind for '@' in URL redaction, (4) undocumented hyper connector bypass in Slack.
Greptile bot provides automated code review and surfaces issues missed in human review — worth checking its summary comments for P2 items after addressing P1 comments.

friction: teloxide's reqwest 0.11 vs workspace reqwest 0.12 version mismatch made Telegram proxy injection impossible — required multiple failed attempts (unsafe set_var, OnceLock, ClientBuilder proxy methods) before accepting the limitation.
Rust 2024 edition + workspace `unsafe_code = deny` blocked the simplest workaround (std::env::set_var) for the Telegram env var approach.
`just lint` and `just test` fail on macOS due to CUDA feature inclusion — had to run crate-specific clippy commands manually.
The `build_schema_map()` validation schema required manually matching the TOML key names — no derive macro or automatic sync with the config struct.
open_items: Per-channel proxy support deferred — the application-level proxy covers the 90% case; per-channel config can layer on top in a follow-up.
Telegram proxy support requires either teloxide upgrading to reqwest 0.12 or a separate proxy mechanism — currently users must set HTTPS_PROXY env var manually.
Slack's chat.postMessage via slack-morphism's hyper connector bypasses the reqwest proxy — would require patching slack-morphism or using a different client approach.
The `just lint`/`just test` CUDA build failure on macOS is a pre-existing issue unrelated to this PR but encountered during development.
build_http_client() silently falls back on bad proxy URLs (logs warn but continues) — could be made more strict in a future pass.
